title: pinus kesiya var. langbianensis
intro: Pinus kesiya var. langbianensis, also known as the Langbian pine, is a variety of pine tree native to Vietnam and Laos. It is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree with a broad, conical crown and long, needle-like leaves that are highly valued for their medicinal properties. The essential oils extracted from the leaves of the Langbian pine have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pinus kesiya var. langbianensis include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions to promote overall well-being.
